title: A Supermarket In California Allen Ginsberg Summary In Hindi
description:
Imagine walking through a fluorescent-lit American supermarket at midnight, haunted by visions of Walt Whitman browsing the produce aisle — that's exactly the electric, grief-soaked journey Allen Ginsberg constructs in *A Supermarket in California*, a poem that turns grocery shopping into a meditation on loneliness, lost America, and queer desire. Written in 1955 as a direct address to Whitman, this Beat Generation masterpiece asks a question every BA English Honours and DU SOL student must wrestle with: what happens to a poet's dream when the world it imagined never arrives? Ginsberg's neon-lit elegy hits harder once you understand what he was really mourning.
